title: Power user: automation wins and multi-entity gaps

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Aisha, you're one of our heaviest users. What do you love about Maple?

turns.speaker: Aisha
turns.text: The automation, hands down. The rules engine is excellent. I set up rules so vendor X always lands in the right category with the right class, and now ninety percent of transactions categorize themselves. That alone saves me a full day a month.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: That's great to hear. Anything else that works well?

turns.speaker: Aisha
turns.text: The receipt-to-transaction matching is smart, and the audit log is clean, which my auditors love. When the product works, it really works. It's clearly built by people who understand bookkeeping.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: Where does it fall short for you?

turns.speaker: Aisha
turns.text: Multi-entity. I run four LLCs and Maple really wants me to treat them as one company. I have to keep four separate logins and I can't see a consolidated view across them. Switching accounts all day is maddening.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: What would the ideal look like?

turns.speaker: Aisha
turns.text: One login, an entity switcher, and a consolidated dashboard that rolls up all four. Plus a real approval workflow, right now approvals are just an email nudge. I want multi-step approvals with thresholds, like anything over five thousand needs my sign-off.

turns.speaker: Marcus
turns.text: If multi-entity isn't solved, does that put your account at risk?

turns.speaker: Aisha
turns.text: It's the one thing that could push me to look elsewhere, because it's a daily friction. But honestly the automation is so good that I keep tolerating the login juggling. Fix multi-entity and approvals and I'd be a customer for life.
